lane_4_tenant_token_endpoint.proxy_issue_suspected: PARTIAL — proxy present but did not block; SDK may be using proxy when it should not for internal API calls
lane_4_tenant_token_endpoint.note: Python urllib.request reached endpoint in 1.05s via proxy; SDK stdio process may route differently

lane_5_tool_reproduction.error_message: Missing access token for authorization. Please make a request with token attached.
lane_5_tool_reproduction.stderr_evidence: Error invoking MCP tool 'lark_im_v1_chat_list' via sync wrapper

supporting_evidence: HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY both set to http://127.0.0.1:10808 in Python process environment
LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY is NOT set — Lark MCP stdio subprocess inherits proxy env vars
NO_PROXY is MISSING — open.feishu.cn is not in no-proxy list
SDK's HTTP client may route API calls through proxy when it should bypass for internal API endpoints
4.07s elapsed (vs 1.05s Python urllib test) suggests SDK path slower, possibly proxy retry or timeout
Lark CLI Adapter (lark_cli_adapter.ts) sets LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY=1 to disable proxy — confirming project aware of proxy issue for CLI
Python urllib test used proxy successfully to reach endpoint — but SDK's node.js environment may handle proxy differently
disconfirming_evidence: Python urllib.request successfully reached endpoint via proxy (200 status in 1.05s)
SDK in tenant mode loads tools and initializes (17 tools) — network connectivity exists
keytar warning seen but keytar is NOT involved in tenant token flow per R147D analysis
lane_6_root_cause.sdk_token_attach_analysis: SDK's larkOapiHandler calls func(params) without token when shouldUseUAT=false. Client.request() should auto-fetch tenant token. If proxy routes call incorrectly or SDK's HTTP agent doesn't use proxy correctly for token endpoint, token fetch fails silently. The SDK then calls API without token, resulting in 99991661.

lane_7_repair_options.name: Add NO_PROXY=open.feishu.cn to Lark MCP env
lane_7_repair_options.description: Add 'env': {'NO_PROXY': 'open.feishu.cn'} to lark entry in extensions_config.json. This bypasses proxy for open.feishu.cn domain, allowing SDK to reach token endpoint directly.
lane_7_repair_options.reason: Lowest-risk fix: adds one env var to lark MCP config. If proxy is causing SDK's token fetch to fail, this should resolve it. Lark CLI Adapter already uses LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY=1 confirming proxy awareness.

lane_7_repair_options.name: Add LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY=1 to Lark MCP env
lane_7_repair_options.description: Add 'env': {'LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY': '1'} to lark entry — same pattern as lark_cli_adapter.ts uses to disable proxy for CLI commands.
lane_7_repair_options.reason: LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY=1 disables proxy for lark-cli but Lark MCP is a different tool. May not have same effect on SDK's HTTP calls.

lane_7_repair_options.name: Fix keytar native dependency for OAuth flow
lane_7_repair_options.description: Rebuild/reinstall keytar so AuthStore can persist OAuth user tokens. Bypass tenant token flow entirely.
lane_7_repair_options.reason: keytar fix is high-risk (may not fix SDK issue) and requires dependency install which is prohibited in diagnostic phase

lane_7_repair_options.name: Pause Lark, investigate Tavily 405
lane_7_repair_options.description: Disable Lark MCP, re-enable Tavily, investigate R131A Tavily 405 error
lane_7_repair_options.reason: Tavily 405 investigation is independent and may be simpler. But Option A is lower risk and worth trying first.

validation_plan: 1. Patch extensions_config.json: add env.NO_PROXY=open.feishu.cn to lark entry
5. Expect: [] (bot not in groups) or list of chat objects
6. If error 99991661: proxy fix insufficient — try keytar fix or pause Lark
7. If other error: inspect and classify
8. Record: tool execution success/failure, error code, response shape, elapsed time
lane_8_r147g_authorization_package.rollback_plan: Remove NO_PROXY from extensions_config.json env field

lane_9_mcp_branch_decision.rationale: R147F identified a specific, low-risk fix: add NO_PROXY=open.feishu.cn to lark MCP env. This addresses the most likely root cause (proxy/no-proxy conflict). Option A is non-invasive and can be validated in one smoke test. Only if R147G fails should we pause Lark and investigate Tavily.

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY set to localhost:10808 in Python process; SDK stdio subprocess inherits these; NO_PROXY missing means open.feishu.cn not excluded; SDK's HTTP client may route token fetch through proxy causing it to fail silently; Python urllib test reached endpoint via proxy successfully but SDK node.js may handle proxy differently
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147F: proxy/no-proxy conflict identified as primary hypothesis; R147G authorized to add NO_PROXY=open.feishu.cn to lark env

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: lark_cli_adapter.ts sets LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY=1 to disable proxy for lark-cli commands, confirming project team is aware of proxy impact on Lark tooling
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147F: same NO_PROXY pattern should apply to Lark MCP stdio subprocess

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Python urllib.request reached tenant token endpoint via proxy in 1.05s with 200 status; proves network path exists; but SDK node.js HTTP agent may behave differently
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147F: SDK may need explicit NO_PROXY setting to bypass proxy for internal API calls
